title: InstCombine: Check one use before trying to simplify copysign sign
summary:
The generalization extends the copysign sign-bit simplification to handle bitcasts with multiple uses, not just constant expressions. The original pattern only matched `copysign(mag, bitcast(C))` where `C` is a constant. The patch relaxes this by allowing the bitcast source to have multiple uses, as long as the bitcast itself has exactly one use (by the copysign). This enables the optimization to apply in more real-world scenarios where the sign-bit integer value may be computed and reused elsewhere, as shown in the new test cases.

Key insight: The sign bit is extracted via demanded bits analysis on the integer source; other uses of that source don't affect the sign-bit isolation. The `m_OneUse` constraint on the bitcast ensures we don't increase instruction count unnecessarily. Developers can apply this pattern when an operation only depends on specific bits (like the sign bit) and the bitwise operation producing those bits can be analyzed independently of other uses.
